titlestmt.titleproper: National Federation of Grain Cooperatives records2940
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

p.subject: {**Subject Code, Use collection subject browse list}

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-03-01 18:13:27 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: National Federation of Grain Cooperatives records
did.abstract: Legislation; congressional committee hearings, comments, and press releases regarding agriculture law and legislation; press releases of farmers' associations; correspondence concerning laws, taxation, etc.; material on cooperatives and associations; records of commodity prices; newsletters, reports, memos, etc. concerning import quotas; material on the Uniform Grain Storage Agreement; and other records of agricultural associations and laws and regulations affecting agriculture. Persons named in the records include Martin Abraham Abrahamsen, Dean Acheson, George David Aiken, Carl Bert Albert, John A. Baker, Louis Hyman Bean, Ezra Taft Benson, Walter C. Berger, John Bird, Mortimer Maxwell Caplin, John Alexander Carroll, J.M. Chambers, Willard W. Cochrane, Lloyd H. Davis, Douglas Dillon, Robert J. Dole, Paul Douglas, Milton Stover Eisenhower, Paul L. Farris, William Farris, Orville L. Freeman, Guy Mark Gillette, H.D. Godfrey, Roy F. Hendrickson, Hubert Humphrey, Dr. A. Ladru Jensen, C. Warner Johnson, Edwin Clark Johnson, Lyndon B. Johnson, Robert Samuel Kerr, William Leonard Langer, Catharine May, Fred Maywald, Eugene J. McCarthy, Wilbur D. Mills, Karl E. Mundt, Charles S. Murphy, Lawrence F. O'Brien, Albert H. Quie, Don L. Short, Edward John Thye, Thomas F. Toohey, Horace Jeremiah Voorhis, Henry Agard Wallace, John Clarence Watts, and Jamie L. Whitten.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Legislation; congressional committee hearings, comments, and press releases regarding agriculture law and legislation; press releases of farmers' associations; correspondence concerning laws, taxation, etc.; material on cooperatives and associations; records of commodity prices; newsletters, reports, memos, etc. concerning import quotas; material on the Uniform Grain Storage Agreement; and other records of agricultural associations and laws and regulations affecting agriculture. Persons named in the records include Martin Abraham Abrahamsen, Dean Acheson, George David Aiken, Carl Bert Albert, John A. Baker, Louis Hyman Bean, Ezra Taft Benson, Walter C. Berger, John Bird, Mortimer Maxwell Caplin, John Alexander Carroll, J.M. Chambers, Willard W. Cochrane, Lloyd H. Davis, Douglas Dillon, Robert J. Dole, Paul Douglas, Milton Stover Eisenhower, Paul L. Farris, William Farris, Orville L. Freeman, Guy Mark Gillette, H.D. Godfrey, Roy F. Hendrickson, Hubert Humphrey, Dr. A. Ladru Jensen, C. Warner Johnson, Edwin Clark Johnson, Lyndon B. Johnson, Robert Samuel Kerr, William Leonard Langer, Catharine May, Fred Maywald, Eugene J. McCarthy, Wilbur D. Mills, Karl E. Mundt, Charles S. Murphy, Lawrence F. O'Brien, Albert H. Quie, Don L. Short, Edward John Thye, Thomas F. Toohey, Horace Jeremiah Voorhis, Henry Agard Wallace, John Clarence Watts, and Jamie L. Whitten.

emph.ref: Series I. Series I. Agricultural Act
Series II. Series II. Co-op Taxes
Series III. Series III. Import Quotas
Series IV. Series IV. Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA)
Series V. Series V. Assorted Materials
Series VI. Series VI. Chronological Files

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: National Federation of Grain Cooperatives records, #2940. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Agricultural Act: "Prosperity and Progress for the Farmer and Rural America"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pres LBJ, statement, 18pp, plus summary statement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Legislative bills

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Congress Record, Senate and House debates and comments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings; press releases; bills; memos; statements; House Committee on Agriculture; wheat; Secretary of Agriculture; Senate Committee on Agricultre and Forestry; Bob Dole, M.C., Kansas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies, Public Law 88-297,regarding cotton and wheat (multiple copies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: House Committee on Agriculture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Public Law 88-26, Feed Grain Act (copies); hearings; Feed Grain Act 1963; miscellaneous statements and reports regarding agriculture and wheat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence; memo.; regarding the act; Hubert Humphrey; Milton R. Young; National Council of Farmer Cooperatives; Kerr Grain Corporation, Oregon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills and messages regarding Act of 1962

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills; statements; correspondence regarding 1962 Act: Roy F. Hendrickson statement; Orville L. Freeman before Senate Committee on Agriculture and Forestry; National Association of Wheat Growers, Glen L. Bayne, President; National Council of Farmer Cooperatives; Press Release, National Farmers Union

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills regarding Act of 1962

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills, mostly, regarding Act of 1961

did.unittitle: Agricultural Act -- USDA, President J. Kennedy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Testimony, statements, memo., press releases, regarding Act of 1961; O.L. Freeman; "Last working draft" Administration Confidential Agricultural Bill; Testimony, O.L. Freeman, Senate Agricultural Committee; E.M. Norton, national Milk Producers Federation before Senate Committee on Agriculture and Forestry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statements regarding: Cooperative League of the USA, Jerry Voorhis to Senator Paul Douglas, letter, Canton, South Dakota, regarding legislation; Albert Lowenfels, NY State; National Association of Wheat Growers, before House Committee on Agriculture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Farm Bill, either positive, negative, neutral

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Wheat Marketing Act of 1960" description, 5 pp. memo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press releases, statements regarding National Farmers Union, House Subcommittee on Wheat; Pres. Eisenhower's Message to Congree; Farm Legislation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Congressional record; bills; statements; press releases regarding Act; Gordon Roth; memos regarding surplus, wheat, and House Representative Edward J. Thye, Minnesota

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills, statements, press releases regarding Act

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, press releases, statements regarding Act; M.W. Thatcher, President, NFGC to Roy F. Hendrickson St. Paul

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press releases, reports, clippings, correspondence regarding 1954; includes "Appraisal of the Agricultural Acts of 1948 and 1949 … by Ezra T. Benson … (by) Iowa State College …" Mimeograph( June 4, 1953) 8 pp.; correspondents: Edward J. Thye, Senate, Minnesota, Harold D. Cooley, House, N. Caroline, R.F.H to Clifford R. Hope, House, Kansas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Congressional record, clippings, correspondence regarding Act; USDA Milton R. Young, Senate, N. Dakota (copy of letter Apr. 14, 1953) regarding parity prices

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills regarding Act

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly correspondence including Harold D. Cooley, House, N. Carolina regarding parity; Milton R. Young, Senate, N. Dakota regarding "Young-Russell" bill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text of the Act

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text of the Act; also Roy F. Hendrickson to M.W. Thatcher on the Act (Nov. 6, 1948); 2 pp. plus 10 pp. comments on agricultural situation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes registration list of attendees and 3 copies "Legislative Background of the New Tax Treatment of Cooperatives and their Patrons" by John E. Noakes, New York (Nov. 19-20, 1962), Washington D.C. 9 pp. + 2 pp. footnotes

did.unittitle: Co-op Taxes, H R 13103 -- Foreign Investors Tax Act of 1966

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bill and Roy F. Hendrickson letters to Eugene McCarthy, Senate, Minnesota, and Frank Carlson, Senate, Kansas regarding section 212 which relates to cooperatives

did.unittitle: Co-op Taxes, Cooperative Marketing Association, Kansas City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly correspondence regarding IRS and CMA and income tax exemption status

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of Cache Valley Case, 1965; copy of "Farmers' Cooperative Income Tax Returns for 1953" IRS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding cooperatives and taxation with Kansas Farmers' Service Association and memo from Nieman and Bosard, Attorneys, Minneapolis regarding "the question of income tax on government business"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Protest and covering letter regarding taxing of copperatives with protest from The Cooperative Elevator and Supply Company, Meade, Kansas, 23 pp., Confidential

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly correspondence regarding Interal Revenue and cooperatives, and matter of capital retains

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes memos from Roy F. Hendrickson to Directors; and copies of Federal Register

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Telegrams, resolutions, regarding cooperative taxes; National Council of Farmer Cooperatives, Homer L. Brinkely, W.E. Rumble (St. Paul, Attorneys), Cooperative League of the United States, Jerry Voorhis (Ex Director)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: NFGC Directors regarding amendment to HR 10650, Texas, Kansas, Iowa; Correspondence with Nebraksa, New York, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, W.E. Rumble (Apr 16, 1962) before Senate, Committee on Finance, 10 pp. mimeograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly correspondence to and from Dr. A Ladru Jensen, Attorney, Salt Lake City regarding taxation of cooperatives including "Summary of Revenue Act of Oct 1962 As it Applies to Cooperatives" 5 pp.; also 6 pp. mimeograph statement/letter from Ladru to Senator Wallace F. Bennett (May 7, 1962) regarding HR 10650; National Rural Electric Cooperative Association; National Council of Farmer Cooperatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mr. Max M. Kampelman, Attorney, Washington, memo to Senator Eugene McCarthy (June 16, 1962), 6 pp., plus 2 pp. supplemental (copy) memo is regarding HR 10650 and taxation of cooperatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of letter sent to Senators Robert S. Kerr, Eugene McCarthy, Frank Carlson, Carl T. Curtis regarding Senator Clinton Anderson proposal of taxing cooperatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: National Council of Farmer Cooperatives and HR 10650 correspondence and memos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some literature of the National Tax Equality Association (1962) and article of taxing cooperatives which appeared in the READERS DIGEST (Feb 1962)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly statement, Douglas Dillon, Ways and Means Committee (May 3, 1961), subject is taxation; also Roy F. Hendrickson newsletter on taxes (Apr 21, 1961); and press release, White House, "Message on Taxation" (Apr 20, 1961)

did.unittitle: Co-op Taxes - House, Ways and Means Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Roy F. Hendrickson to Wilbur D. Mills, House, and also RGH newsletter (Jan 20, 1959); and Frank G Rankin, Manager, Golf Proof Elevator, Louisville, Kentucky to Roy F. Hendrickson (Nov 20, 1958) regarding Representative John C. Watts, Kentucky, and Mr. Clarence L. Miller, Assistant Secretary of Agriculture, regarding their stand on cooperatives (Watts and Miller) "good for agriculture"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, W.E. Rumble before Ways and Means Committee, mimeograph, 25 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, W.E. Rumble, Ways and Means Committee, 6 pp., mimeograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, W.E. Rumble, Taxation and cooperatives, 29 pp. mimeo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, Congressional Record regarding taxation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, Congressional Record regarding taxation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement of Karl D. Loos (Feb 23, 1950) before Ways and Means Committee, 35 pp., 3 tables, mimeo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, other items regarding hearing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roy F. Hendrickson, newsletter, 30 pp. , mimeograph regarding IRS proposed regulations regarding cooperative taxes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence (2 letters) between Roy F. Hendrickson and Robert C. Guenzel, Attorney, Lincoln regarding cooperatives and IRS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bill, HR 4370 (Feb 4, 1965)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some correspondence and briefs regarding Northwest Agricultural Cooperative Association v. ICC, Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals (Aug 10, 1965)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly correspondence regarding letter Mr. Lester R. McDonald, Farmers Elevator and Merchandise Company, Satanta, Kansas to Representative Floyd Breeding, 5th District, Kansas (May 16, 1958) regarding taxation of cooperatives that they should be taxed; Roy F. Hendrickson assisted in reply from Breeding to McDonald

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, clippings, pamphlets; includes letter, James G. Patton to Roy F. Hendrickson (Mar 19 1956) saying they should visit tax problems and "concerned that we may have some substantially punitive legislation if this crows stays in office"; Confidential Report on Conference on United Action for Tax Justice, Chicago (Oct 12, 1956), 6 pp. mimeo; Roy F. Hendrickson (May 24, 1956) Draft of Proposed Tax Amendment (Confidential) to Directors; Senator Hubert H. Humphrey to Roy F. Hendrickson; (Dec. 30, 1955) regarding taxation of cooperatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports, bills regarding tax legislation; includes Homer L. Brinkley to Roy F.H. and others (Mar 16, 1956) on meeting with members; Ways and Means (Cooper, Mills, Boggs, and Daniel A. Reed) states their feelings on current legislation before them; 2pp confidential

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and draft of proposed tax legislation from National Council of Farmer Cooperatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, newsletters, clippings regarding taxation of cooperatives

did.unittitle: Co-op Taxes - Commodity Credit Corporation grain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memos, reports regarding Commodity Credit Corporation; Roy F. Hendrickson, Fred Maywald, Farmers Grain Dealers Association, Des Moines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ways and Means Committee hearings; W.E. Rumble and Roy F. Hendrickson some good letters regarding hearings (Jan 1958); some copies of statements, some publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence; copies from Congressional Record

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Westcentral and Maywood (Nebraska) cooperative tax cases

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commodity prices

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dan H. McVey, Chief, Grain Branch, USDA, 6 mimeograph reports on grain cooperatives throughout the U.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 mimeo, reports on grain cooperatives, United States

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memos, editorials; series of memos and letters to M.W. Thatcher, General Manager, Farmers Union Grain Terminal Association, St. Paul regarding Canada and grain imports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, newsletters, reports regarding imports of barley; Roy F. Hendrickson to Senators Milton R. Young, Karl E. Mundt, Edward J. Thye (Jan 17, 1954) a super memo on the situation; also reply from Mundt and subsequent eltter of Roy F. Hendrickson to Mundt (Jan 26, 1954) regarding barley import situation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence includes Milton R. Young, George D. Aiken, Francis Case and statement of M.W. Thatcher, St. Paul (Apr 23, 1953) before Senate Committee on Agriculture and Forestry; also testimony, C.L. Dickinson, Grange League Federation (Apr 23, 1953)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Roy F. Hendrickson to Non. Franklin Dow, Customs (Dec 3 1952) 6pp. Letter on definition of wheat, unfit for human consumption

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 2 copies of USDA Regulations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 pp. letter-statement of M.W. Thatcher (May 17, 1954) regarding imports of grain and the Agricultural Adjustment Act sent to numerous Senators and Congressmen and some replies to Thatcher statement - including Senators Hubert H. Humphrey, Milton R. Young, William Langer; also, report of US Government regarding Act (Oct 1955) 23 pp. mimeo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Secretary of Agriculture, Charles F. Brannen (Sep 10, 1952); also letter True D. Morse, and from some members of Congress, as Milton R. Young and Edward J. Thye

did.unittitle: Import Quotas - Wallace letter regarding Feed grains

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of letter Henry A. Wallace, to Secretary of Treasurery (Feb 1, 1953) 3 pp. regarding "unfit for human consumption" and Roy F. Hendrickson sending it to Congressmen in 1954

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roy F. Hendrickson (Dec 21, 1954) to Walter C. Berger, Commodity Stabilization Service, 3pp on durum wheat and some comments from Congressmen regarding that letter and situation in Congress

did.unittitle: Wheat Unfit for Human Consumption - S 1313 Bill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Roy F. Hendrickson and Congressman regarding NFGC and their support for the bill, includes H. Carl Anderson, House, Minnesota and Milton R. Young and Don L. Short

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence includes Roy F. Hendrickson to Karl D. Loos, Solicitor, USDA (Jan 26, 1954) on unfit wheat, letters and reports; E.J. Bell, Grain and Feed Division, USDA (Feb 11, 1965) to Roy F. Hendrickson on Canadian wheat; clippings; statement, Roy F. Hendrickson (Jun 10, 1953), Senate Judiciary Committee on S. 784 unfit wheat; 12 pp. mimeo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, reports and clippings regarding oats and imports; Roy F. Hendrickson to Milton R. Young (Sep 1, 1953) and Young to Roy F. Hendrickson (Aug 25, 1953) on quotas and also general farm situation; several statements, including Prof L.C. Cunningham, Cornell (Jul 9, 1953) regarding importation of oats; 3 pp. plus tables; "Investigation of Importation of Oats from Canada," USDA, about 30 pp., mimeograph (Oct 15, 1952)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement and correspondence on rye and imports; Roy F. Hendrickson statement (Jun 15, 1955), 6 pp, US Tariff Commission; correspondence on rye situation and includes Hubert H. Humphrey, Milton R. Young, Lawrence F. O'Brien, Dean Acheson, Karl E. Mundt, Edward J. Thye

did.unittitle: Imports of Canadian Grain - Defense Production Act

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, statements, bills, public laws; Roy F. Hendrickson statement (May 29, 1952), 4 pp., tables, mimeo; letters from Congressmen indicating receipt of Roy F. Hendrickson letter on import of grain; reference to article CORNELL LAW REVIEW by Edwin G. Martin on trade agreements and price supports, (ca. 1951-1952); Roy F. Hendrickson to M.W. Thatcher (Jun 6, 1952) on amendments where H spells out why amendments lost; see also Roy F. Hendrickson to Mr. James R. Bliss, Lansing, Michigan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, Roy F. Hendrickson 9pp., tables, mimeo, 3 copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, Roy F. Hendrickson 8 pp., tables, 2 copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, Roy F. Hendrickson, Senate Agriculture Committee, 7 pp., mimeo, 3 copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, M.W. Thatcher, 7 pp., Senate Committee on Agriculture and Forestry, 1 copy

did.unittitle: Imports of Flaxseed and Linseed Oil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, Roy F. Hendrickson, 2 pp., U.S. Tariff Commission, 1 copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roy F. Hendrickson to Marvin L. McLain, Commodity Stabilization Service, USDA, 2 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, news release includes Milton R. Young (Apr 10, 1954) on barley situation and on the Secretary Henry A. Wallace letter (Feb 1, 1953) which he says "I can make good use of the material contained in the Secretary's letter." also Congressman N. Dakota with USDA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, Roy F. Hendrickson, 10 pp., U.S. Tariff Commission, 1 copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, Roy F. Hendrickson, 8 pp., Weed Society of America, 1 copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence on "screenings" include Mr. William H. Gee, Seattle, Washington; USDA 1955 grain screenings; Clifford R. Hope, House, Kansas, 1947 import of grain

did.unittitle: Imports of Canadian Wheat, Unfit for Human Consumption Report, Bureau of Customs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fiscal year ended Jun 30, 1952; relating to importation of Wheat Classified as Unfit for Human Consumption, 16 pp., mimeograph, 2 copies

did.unittitle: Imports of Wheat Unfit for Human Consumption Statement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roy F. Hendrickson, 7 pp., mimeograph Senate Committee on Agriculture and Forestry, 1 copy

did.unittitle: Imports of Canadian Wheat, Unfit for Human Consumption Statement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roy F. Hendrickson, 8 pp., Senate Judiciary Committee, 2 copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence on hearings on unfit wheat and the definition of "unfit"; numerous Congressman correspondents; Milton R. Young, Herman Welker, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly information on early 1950s

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Wheat and Wheat Flour" Report 145, Second Series, U.S. Tariff Commission, Washington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, storing grain in Texas; includes Frank M. Phariss, Producers Grain Corporation, Amarillo (Jan 7, 1966) to Roy F. Hendrickson, personal storage, 2 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, forms, reports, tables on storage of grain; Farmers Grain Dealers Association of Iowa; Cooperative Marketing Association, Kansas City, Missouri; Union Equity Co-Operative Exchange, Oklahoma; Westcentral Cooperative Grain Company, Omaha

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA)- Syminton Investigating Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes reports and statements; Roy F. Hendrickson statement; about 15 pp. of tables

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) - Reports of Proceedings, Committee of Investigation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, grain storage with committee and USDA

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) - Proceedings, Vol 2.
Draft Report of Syminton Subcommittee, Grain Storage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports and statements on hearings, Hubert H. Humphrey to Roy F. Hendrickson on Syminton Subcommittee

did.unittitle: Senate Agriculture Committee - Investigation of Grain Storage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, various newspapers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence on grain storage; includes carbon copies and other copies concerning M.W. Thatcher, Roy F. Hendrickson, Hubert H. Humphrey, James G. Patton, and Charles F. Brannan all on Syminton Subcomittee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rate schedules

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) - Final and Proposed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts and final copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: USDA announcements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and minutes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes, meeting, Kansas City; also statements, as Grain and Feed Dealers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newsletters, Roy F. Hendrickson comments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence; includes J. Frank, Producers Grain Corporation, Amarillo; W.A. Richards, North Pacific Grain Growers, Inc., A. Price Feuquay, Enid, Oklahoma; William F. Brooks, National Grain Trade Council; P.J. Nash, Farmers Union Jobbing Association, Kansas City, Missouri

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) Statements, Reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fred Maywald Correspondence; F.M., Manager, Farmers Grain Dealers Association, Des Moines

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) - Commodity Credit Corporation, Storage Cost Figures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports, etc.; some correspondence on Syminton Subcommittee Investigation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and clippings; Kansas, Nebraska

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) - Syminton Subcommittee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Government Accountability Office Report

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) - Proceedings, Vol 1., Syminton Subcommittee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, statements, Syminton Subcommittee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fred Maywald correspondence; also some Congressional correspondence; Don L. Short, N. Dakota; Catherine May, Washington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Guy M. Gillette, Senator, Iowa

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) - Commodity Credit Corporation, Cost Survey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mimeograph, grain storage, USDA, 3 vols.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports, tables

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports, drafts on proposed storage provisions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, newsletters, tables, concerning storage; Oregon, Ohio, Iowa, National Farmers Union; interesting letters between Wes McCune, Farmers Union, and Roy H. Hendrickson on storage and Syminton Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, newsletters, reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports; letters from Nebraska, Texas, and Kansas cooperatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports, hearings, statements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning USDA, correspondence, reports

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) - Congressional Records and Appropriations Bill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills, drafts of legislation, statements

did.unittitle: Uniform Grain Storage Agreement (UGSA) - Martin Abrahamsen
Adomeit, Bernard L. - Illinois Grain Corporation, Letter to Roy F. Hendrickson
Aeration of Grain in Commercial Storage - Proceedings, 1 Vol, Ames, Iowa
Agricultural Adjustment Act, USDA, Agriculture Handbook No. 49

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers presented by staff, USDA, mimeograph

did.unittitle: "Agricultural Policy in an Affluent Society" by Vernon W. Ruttan, University of Minnesota

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Between Andrews, Alamo, Texas, and Roy F. Hendrickson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statements, bills, correspondence on hearings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, press news release, clippings, draft statement by Oriville Freeman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, news release

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills, news release

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills, congressional record, statement regarding appropriations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills
